summarization.a: The figure displays the X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) of MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2,  50cLi-MnO2, MnO2 ref, LiMn2O4 ref, and MnO ref samples.
summarization.b: The figure shows Fourier Transform (FT) of the Mn K-edge extended X-ray absorption near-edge structure (EXAFS) of  MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2,  50cLi-MnO2, MnO2 ref, LiMn2O4 ref, and MnO ref samples.
summarization.c: The figure shows the absorption spectra of MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2,  50cLi-MnO2, MnO2 ref, LiMn2O4 ref, and MnO ref samples, as a function of the energy (eV), highlighting the Mn+3 and Mn+4.

data_extraction.b:
| R(Å) | Intensity (arb. units) |
|---|---|
| 1 | peak at ~1.8 Å |
| 2 | peak at ~2.8 Å |
| 3 | peak at ~3.4 Å |
| 4 | peak at ~4.0 Å |

a.question: How can a reduction in Mn can be observed in the absorption spectra?
a.answer: As can be seen in subfigure a, the reduction of Mn can be observed as it shifts to lower energy values.

a.question: Which sample shows a greater reduction in Mn?
a.answer: The 10cLi-MnO2 samples, due to the peaks are present in lowest energy values.

b.question: How does the Mn-Mn2 decrease as the number of cycles increases?
b.answer: The Mn-Mn2 bond decreases as the number of lithium cycles increases.

b.question: Which sample has the highest Mn-O intensity?
b.answer: The MnO2, 10cLi0MnO2, and 50cLi-Mno2 samples exhibit  the same intensity of Mn-O bonds.
